raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> I received an email about a suspicious charge. {$81.00}. 
I disputed the charge via the phone and in a response to the email. I was told to destroy my existing card ending in XXXX, and I'd be issued a new one. 
As I rarely, if ever used that card in a few years... I didn't give it much more thought. I got an email stating my credit limit had changed XX/XX/XXXX. 
In XXXX we were preparing for a XXXX  month stay in XXXX  ... at which time my mail was forwarded to XXXX. 
I then called to inquire my balance and payed off 98 % of it, still waiting for a result regarding the disputed charge. 
After getting to XXXX  I mentioned to my better half I never received that new card. 
Fast forward to the end of XXXX and I received a letter via XXXX. This letter informing me I owed {$180.00} on a card ending in XXXX. PAST DUE. 
I immediately got on the phone to Citi Bank. After almost XXXX hours of being passed around from this department and that department, I informed each representative about half way through the call they were being recorded. I was MORE than frustrated by this point. 
I finally spoke to a rep that agreed to drop the late fees and I'd just pay the disputed charge, close the account. I was then transferred AGAIN to customer service. 
Where I was then told the late fees couldn't be overridden. I again requested the disputed charge be investigated. And ended up at square 1. I received another email stating I now owe $ XXXX including the fraudulent charge and all late fees. To avoid any derelict marks on my credit report I wrote a check for the minimum due with " under duress '' on the memo line. At this point I don't think I should be responsible for the original disputed charge/late fees/penalties. 
Can you please help me resolve this in a timely manner? My credit score has now dropped XXXX because of this delinquent account Thank You, XXXX XXXX XXXX
